rationale: Pre-registered 3 stratified candidates for the variance pilot (10 seeds x 3). AF-CAND-0007 = older surviving non-benchmark candidate; AF-CAND-0278 = recent day-trading refinement candidate; AF-CAND-0290 = mid-tenure day-trading refinement candidate. All three have committed strategy_spec.json under reports/. AF-CAND-0263 is excluded (locked benchmark, code-enforced guard in scripts/run_ml_variance_pilot.py).

Variance pilot completed cleanly: sigma_pf=0.0083 PF over 10 seeds x 3 stratified candidates on real EUR_USD M1 data (30 runs, ~1.4M cumulative trades). Well under the 0.08 PF hard-exit gate, so no data-collection sub-phase required. Locked mde_pf=0.0083 and effect_size_floor_pf=0.0083 (both = 1x sigma_pf per plan rule 1.6.0.5) into config/eval_gates.toml [ml_variance_pilot]. Required-n at alpha=0.01, power=0.8 = 11 candidates; this becomes the minimum candidate count for Phase 1.6 baseline. Bias self-audit: confirmation_bias_considered=true (used pre-registered candidate set; no post-hoc selection); cherry_picking_considered=true (every seed result included in the report, no exclusions); anchoring_considered=true (sigma_pf measured fresh, not anchored to any prior estimate); complexity_creep_considered=true (script is numpy/pandas/xgboost only; no torch transitively imported, asserted at runtime); sunk_cost_considered=true (would have escalated for data collection if sigma_pf>0.08, regardless of effort already spent); recency_considered=true (candidate set spans recent 0278/0290 plus older 0007); narrative_considered=true (no narrative justification needed for variance-only run); survivorship_considered=true (AF-CAND-0007 chosen explicitly as an older surviving candidate, AF-CAND-0263 excluded as locked benchmark via code-enforced guard).

bias_self_audit.confirmation_bias_note: Used pre-registered candidate set DEC-ML-1.6.0-CANDIDATES; no post-hoc filtering of seeds or candidates.
bias_self_audit.cherry_picking_note: All 30 seed*candidate runs included in report; no outlier removal.
bias_self_audit.anchoring_note: sigma_pf measured fresh from data; no prior expectation seeded into the analysis.
bias_self_audit.complexity_creep_note: Pilot is numpy/pandas/xgboost only; assert_no_torch_import() runs at end of script.
bias_self_audit.sunk_cost_note: Hard exit gate (sigma_pf>0.08) was checked irrespective of effort; would have escalated for data collection if exceeded.
bias_self_audit.recency_note: Candidate set spans tenure: AF-CAND-0007 (older surviving), AF-CAND-0278 and AF-CAND-0290 (recent day-trading refinements).
bias_self_audit.narrative_note: No narrative interpretation applied; report is descriptive statistics only.
bias_self_audit.survivorship_note: AF-CAND-0007 chosen explicitly to include an older surviving candidate; AF-CAND-0263 (locked benchmark) excluded by code-enforced guard in the pilot script.

rationale: Pre-registering 11 stratified candidates for Phase 1.6 baseline comparison BEFORE any results are computed (per cross-cutting principle 12 + plan 1.6 stratification rule). Stratification: 2x recent (AF-CAND-0716 latest, AF-CAND-0738), 2x recent-mid (AF-CAND-0739, AF-CAND-0734), 2x mid-tenure (AF-CAND-0322, AF-CAND-0323), 2x older (AF-CAND-0007 from variance pilot, AF-CAND-0009), 2x oldest non-AF-CAND-0263 (AF-CAND-0001, AF-CAND-0002), 1x marginal (AF-CAND-0290 from variance pilot). AF-CAND-0263 (locked benchmark) excluded by both stratification rule and code-enforced guard in scripts/run_ml_baseline_comparison.py. Required-n=11 from PILOT-1.6.0-20260420 satisfied. Bias self-audit: confirmation_bias_considered=true (pre-registered before results), cherry_picking_considered=true (no post-hoc swaps allowed; if a candidate fails to load, run halts), anchoring_considered=true (selection by tenure not by prior performance), complexity_creep_considered=true (no new features versus pilot), sunk_cost_considered=true (older candidates included to avoid recency bias), recency_considered=true (full tenure span Mar-Apr 2026), narrative_considered=true (selection rule is mechanical, not narrative), survivorship_considered=true (oldest surviving non-benchmark intentionally included).

bias_self_audit.confirmation_bias_note: Candidate set pre-registered in this entry before any 1.6 results computed.
bias_self_audit.cherry_picking_note: Stratification rule fixed; runner halts on missing spec.
bias_self_audit.anchoring_note: Selection by tenure bucket, not by prior PF.
bias_self_audit.complexity_creep_note: Same 11 features as pilot; no torch.
bias_self_audit.sunk_cost_note: Older AF-CAND-0001/2 included even though long history.

rationale: Phase 1.6 baseline comparison completed across the 11 pre-registered stratified candidates from DEC-ML-1.6-CANDIDATES, on dataset SHA 7875ba5a... with sealed holdout HOLDOUT-ML-P2-20260420 (38944 rows, ciphertext SHA 8b6fbd4741c9cb52..., key stored OUTSIDE the repo). Three-fold purged walk-forward CV with embargo=20 bars (>= longest feature lookback per D11). Locked XGBoost hparams from variance pilot. AGGREGATE EDGE CONFIRMED: median PF lift = +0.0506, mean = +0.0458, both ~5.5x the locked effect-size floor of 0.0083. 11/11 candidates exceed the floor (100%) and 11/11 pass the +1.0 pip cost-sensitivity gate. REGIME GATE FAILURE: 5 of 11 candidates (AF-CAND-0716, AF-CAND-0738, AF-CAND-0739, AF-CAND-0009, AF-CAND-0001) show negative PF lift in at least one regime bucket despite positive aggregate. This is the asymmetry Phase 1.6 was designed to expose; per plan section 5 Hard Exit Gates (any regime PF lift < 0 -> ML stack review; P2 candidacy questioned), the verdict is CONDITIONAL not GO. The 6 clean candidates (AF-CAND-0734, AF-CAND-0322, AF-CAND-0323, AF-CAND-0007, AF-CAND-0002, AF-CAND-0290) form the surviving subset that should be carried into Phase 1.6b for the sequential-features probe; the 5 regime-fragile candidates remain in the report but are flagged. Phase 2.0 HITL re-gate will weigh whether sequential features absorb the regime fragility or whether the fragile candidates should be retired pre-P2.

bias_self_audit.confirmation_bias_note: Used pre-registered candidate set; flagged 5 fragile candidates rather than discarding them.
bias_self_audit.cherry_picking_note: All 11 candidates retained in report; surviving subset surfaces the data, not selectively removes it.
bias_self_audit.anchoring_note: Floor 0.0083 inherited from PILOT-1.6.0; not adjusted to make verdict more favorable.
bias_self_audit.complexity_creep_note: No new features over pilot; numpy/pandas/xgboost only; no torch.
bias_self_audit.sunk_cost_note: Verdict downgraded to conditional despite engineering effort, because the data warranted it.
bias_self_audit.recency_note: Recent candidates (0716/0738/0739) over-represented among fragile group; flagged.
bias_self_audit.narrative_note: No narrative reframing of the regime gate failure; reported as observed.
bias_self_audit.survivorship_note: Oldest non-benchmark candidates AF-CAND-0001/0002/0007 included; 0001 fragile, 0002+0007 clean.

rationale: Pre-registering Phase 1.6b primary endpoint and P2 target lift BEFORE running the sequential-features probe (per cross-cutting principle 12 + plan 1.6b.0). Primary endpoint: cross-candidate mean delta-PF lift (sequential 17-feature XGB minus baseline 11-feature XGB) over the 6 surviving non-fragile candidates from DEC-ML-1.6-COMPLETE. P2 target lift = 0.10 PF (a meaningful absolute PF improvement an ML stack would need to produce above the baseline to justify the operational complexity of P2 1D-CNN + 5-seed ensemble + research-only deployment infrastructure). Hard exit gates per plan section 6: aggregate lift >= 50% of target (i.e. >= 0.05 PF) -> CONDITIONAL; lift < 50% -> P2 cancelled or downgraded; no lift -> P2 cancelled. Secondary per-feature significance via BH-FDR at q=0.10. Stationarity: ADF + KPSS per sequential feature; rolling-z-score normalization for any flagged non-stationary. Surviving candidate set (mechanically derived from 1.6 regime gate, NOT post-hoc selected): AF-CAND-0734, AF-CAND-0322, AF-CAND-0323, AF-CAND-0007, AF-CAND-0002, AF-CAND-0290. Bias self-audit: confirmation_bias_considered=true (target locked before run), cherry_picking_considered=true (surviving subset comes from a code-enforced 1.6 gate), anchoring_considered=true (target 0.10 chosen as round meaningful PF, not anchored to expected lift), complexity_creep_considered=true (six features only, no torch), sunk_cost_considered=true (would cancel P2 cleanly if lift fails), recency_considered=true (surviving subset spans full tenure: 0290/0734 recent, 0007 mid-old, 0002 oldest), narrative_considered=true (mechanical), survivorship_considered=true (the survivorship is explicit and audited by the 1.6 regime gate).

bias_self_audit.confirmation_bias_note: Target and primary endpoint locked before probe run.
bias_self_audit.complexity_creep_note: Six deterministic features only; no torch.
bias_self_audit.sunk_cost_note: Will cancel P2 if lift insufficient.
bias_self_audit.survivorship_note: Survivorship explicit via the 1.6 regime gate.

rationale: Sequential-features probe complete on the 6 surviving non-fragile candidates from DEC-ML-1.6-COMPLETE. Primary endpoint cross-candidate mean delta-PF lift = +0.0041 PF, which is +4.1% of the locked P2 target lift of 0.10 PF and below the 50% conditional-go threshold (0.05 PF). One-sided paired t p-value over 18 cand-fold pairs = 0.2387 (not significant). Per-feature secondary endpoints via BH-FDR at q=0.10: zero of 6 features rejected (every p ~ 0.224). Per-candidate breakdown: 0734 +0.0151, 0322 +0.0150, 0323 -0.0181, 0007 +0.0021, 0002 +0.0129, 0290 -0.0025. Regime non-negativity: 6/6 PASS with sequential features. Cost persistence at 1 pip: 6/6 PASS. Stationarity (ADF & KPSS): 6/6 features stationary (no rolling-z normalization required). Per plan section 6 hard-exit gates (lift below 50% of target but above zero), the verdict is p2_proceed_unchanged: sequential features did not earn their seat in the P2 architecture, so P2 should proceed with the originally-architected feature surface and 1D-CNN + 5-seed ensemble plan, NOT a sequential-features detour. The +0.0041 PF mean is below the effect-size floor (0.0083) on its own, so this is not even meaningfully greater than the sigma_PF noise floor measured in 1.6.0 (sigma_PF=0.0083).

bias_self_audit.confirmation_bias_note: Target pre-registered before probe; results reported regardless of direction.
bias_self_audit.cherry_picking_note: All 6 candidates included; no reruns; no winsorizing.
bias_self_audit.anchoring_note: Verdict derived mechanically from the report numbers.
bias_self_audit.complexity_creep_note: Six features tested as a block; no individual cherry-picking via BH-FDR at q=0.10.
bias_self_audit.sunk_cost_note: Sequential features not added despite the scaffolding work; evidence does not justify it.
bias_self_audit.recency_note: Surviving subset spans candidate tenure broadly; no recency bias in lift signal.
bias_self_audit.narrative_note: Verdict from the report numbers, not a narrative.
bias_self_audit.survivorship_note: The surviving subset is itself derived from a code-enforced 1.6 regime gate, pre-registered.

rationale: Pre-registering Phase 2.0 ML candidate set BEFORE the sealed holdout is opened. PRIMARY ENDPOINT cohort (binding, n=6): the six survivors from DEC-ML-1.6-RESULTS that satisfied the regime non-negativity gate AND the cost-persistence gate at +1.0 pip in Phase 1.6: AF-CAND-0734, AF-CAND-0322, AF-CAND-0323, AF-CAND-0007, AF-CAND-0002, AF-CAND-0290. SECONDARY ENDPOINT cohort (non-binding, descriptive, n=11): all 11 candidates from DEC-ML-1.6-CANDIDATES, including the five regime-fragile candidates (AF-CAND-0716, 0738, 0739, 0009, 0001). The survivor cohort is the only set that drives the Phase 2 GO/CONDITIONAL/NO_GO verdict; the secondary cohort exists solely to record the Q1 fragile-strongly-negative diagnostic (rule defined in DEC-ML-2.0-TARGET). EX-2 gate sensitivity report f1c9adf8... was inspected before this entry to verify gates were not tuned to flip 1.6's outcome. Locked: dataset SHA 7875ba5a..., XGB hparams ref src/agentic_forex/ml/variance_pilot.LOCKED_XGB_HPARAMS, holdout id HOLDOUT-ML-P2-20260420.
survivor_selection_basis: Phase 1.6 gates: regime_non_negative AND cost_persistent_at_1pip both true

bias_self_audit.confirmation_bias_note: Survivor cohort selected by deterministic 1.6 gates (regime AND cost) BEFORE the Phase 2 verdict band was set. EX-2 gate-sensitivity report f1c9adf8... documents that the Q1/I1 thresholds in DEC-ML-2.0-TARGET would yield PRIMARY_OK on 1.6 evidence; the Q1 rule was selected by analogy to standard sigma-level translations, NOT chosen to fail-or-pass against any specific 1.6 distribution. The survivors-vs-fragiles split is a regime-quality split, not a magnitude split (fragile mean lift 0.0494 was actually higher than survivor mean 0.0428 in 1.6).
bias_self_audit.cherry_picking_note: Primary cohort is exactly the union of {regime_non_negative AND cost_persistent_at_1pip} from 1.6 results; no candidates added or removed by judgement. If a survivor strategy_spec.json fails to load at Phase 2 runtime, the runner halts (does not silently swap).
bias_self_audit.anchoring_note: Pre-registration occurs before any sealed-holdout decryption (cap=2 events, currently 0). No prior Phase 2 OOS PF estimate exists to anchor against; the 0.10 PF target was set independently in DEC-ML-2.0-TARGET based on the EX-1 MDE band, not by inspecting any P2 result.
bias_self_audit.complexity_creep_note: Same 11 features as Phase 1.6 (BASELINE_FEATURE_COLUMNS); no torch transitively imported in the primary endpoint pipeline. Sequential-feature variants are explicitly excluded by DEC-ML-1.6b-A1-AUTHORIZATION. Locked XGB hparams unchanged from Phase 1.6.0 pilot.
bias_self_audit.sunk_cost_note: If Q1 rule fires NO_GO on the secondary cohort or if I1 had yielded TIER_3, Phase 2 would have been blocked even though Phases 1.6.0/1.6/1.6b/1.7 are complete. The hard cap of 2 holdout decryptions is enforced by tools/holdout_access_ceremony.py regardless of effort already spent.
bias_self_audit.recency_note: Survivor cohort spans the full tenure: AF-CAND-0007 (older, from variance pilot), AF-CAND-0734/0322/0323 (mid-tenure), AF-CAND-0002 (oldest non-benchmark surviving), AF-CAND-0290 (recent day-trading refinement). The recency-vs-survivorship trade-off is documented and balanced.
bias_self_audit.narrative_note: Cohort is mechanically derived from 1.6 gate results; no narrative interpretation applied. The entry rationale is descriptive of the selection rule, not justificatory of a preferred outcome.
bias_self_audit.survivorship_note: AF-CAND-0001 (oldest non-benchmark, 1.6 fragile) is intentionally tracked in the secondary cohort as a survivorship-bias check. AF-CAND-0263 (locked benchmark) is excluded by both AGENTS.md governance and code-enforced guard.

rationale: Pre-registering the Phase 2.0 verdict bands, primary endpoint, secondary endpoint, statistical procedure, and R4-11 mid-process prediction scaffold BEFORE the sealed holdout is opened. This is the binding pre-commitment; deviations after holdout open invalidate the Phase 2 verdict and force NO_GO. PRIMARY ENDPOINT: aggregate PF lift of XGB-vs-rule on the n=6 survivor cohort (DEC-ML-2.0-CANDIDATES primary set), measured on the sealed holdout HOLDOUT-ML-P2-20260420 ciphertext after governed decryption (max 2 events). Verdict bands: GO if observed_lift >= 0.10 PF AND BCa-95% CI lower bound > 0; CONDITIONAL if 0.055 <= observed_lift < 0.10 PF (Option A: above MDE_upper=0.0503 band, rounded up for clarity); NO_GO otherwise OR if Q1 fragile-strongly-negative rule fires. The 0.055 PF CONDITIONAL floor was chosen as the next clean round number above the MDE upper-CI bound from EX-1 (mde_pf_at_upper_ci_bound=0.0503) so the band is unambiguously distinguishable from MDE noise. The 0.10 PF target equals 4.7x sigma_cross and is the stretch-but-justifiable level the analyst pre-committed to. COMPARATOR: baseline-XGB-on-survivors-only as defined in src/agentic_forex/ml/baseline_runner.py:evaluate_candidate at locked XGB hparams; this is a HARDER comparator than rule-baseline because it tests the residual P2 architecture lift over an already-best-effort XGB on the same survivors. Choosing this comparator is itself a confirmation-bias mitigation (see audit). Q1 RULE: applied to the n=11 SECONDARY cohort only (descriptive). CONDITIONAL_RESTRICTED if mean_fragile_lift < -1*sigma_cross (-0.0211); NO_GO if mean_fragile_lift < -2*sigma_cross (-0.0422) AND >=3/5 fragile candidates show negative lift. Both thresholds are CODE-ENFORCED via the allowlist in tools/gate_sensitivity.py SURVIVORS/FRAGILES frozensets and verified by tests/test_gate_sensitivity.py. STATISTICAL PROCEDURE: BCa moving-block bootstrap on per-trade outcomes; block_size = max(20 bars, sqrt(n_trades)/4); n_resamples = 10000; bootstrap_rng_seed = 20260420 (matches EX-1). Bonferroni family for the regime sub-tests = 4 sub-tests at family alpha=0.01, per-test alpha=0.0025; the PRIMARY aggregate test is uncorrected (single primary endpoint). I1 LOCKED TIER (from EX-1): TIER_1_PROCEED (mde_pf_at_upper_ci_bound=0.0503 <= 0.10). R4-11 MID-PROCESS PREDICTION SCAFFOLD: two predictions logged in Goblin/decisions/predictions.jsonl per Goblin/decisions/PREDICTIONS_SCHEMA.md: (a) MIDPOINT prediction at first commit producing non-error PF on non-holdout for the P2 architecture, (b) TRIGGER prediction immediately before the sealed-holdout decryption ceremony. Both must record predicted_verdict, predicted_point_estimate_pf, predicted_ci_low/high, commit_sha_at_prediction, rationale_note (>=50 chars), predictor_attestation (>=30 chars). The DEC-ML-2.0-RE-GATE entry will surface midpoint and trigger predictions side-by-side with the actual holdout result; large midpoint-to-trigger drift is itself flagged as evidence of in-development belief drift / implicit peeking.
conditional_band_basis: Option A: next clean round number above EX-1 mde_pf_at_upper_ci_bound (0.0503)
comparator_rationale_for_confirmation_bi: Comparing P2 architecture against an already-strong XGB-on-survivors comparator (rather than the rule baseline) deliberately raises the bar: any positive P2 lift over this comparator is a true architectural-residual gain. This makes confirmation of the P2 architecture HARDER, not easier.

bias_self_audit.confirmation_bias_note: Three explicit mitigations: (1) Comparator is XGB-on-survivors not rule-baseline, raising the bar for the P2 architecture; (2) The 0.055 PF CONDITIONAL floor was chosen as a clean round number above MDE_upper, NOT to make any specific anticipated point-estimate land in CONDITIONAL; (3) EX-2 gate sensitivity report f1c9adf8... documents that the chosen Q1/I1 thresholds yield PRIMARY_OK on 1.6 evidence, proving the rules are not a post-hoc fit. The R4-11 midpoint+trigger predictions are the strongest mitigation: pre-committed predictions held against actual holdout outcome are the audit trail for confirmation bias.
bias_self_audit.cherry_picking_note: All evaluation parameters (target, conditional band, Q1 thresholds, Bonferroni family, block size, n_resamples, RNG seed, comparator) are LOCKED in this entry. Any deviation post-decryption invalidates the verdict per .github/workflows/holdout-ceremony.yml and tools/holdout_access_ceremony.py auditing.
bias_self_audit.anchoring_note: 0.10 PF target = 4.7x sigma_cross, set independently of any anticipated P2 result. Conditional band = MDE_upper + small clean buffer (0.055), not anchored to any 1.6 estimate. Q1 thresholds = standard sigma-level translations.
bias_self_audit.complexity_creep_note: Statistical procedure is BCa moving-block bootstrap with codified parameters; no model-selection or hyper-tuning step is performed at evaluation time. Bonferroni family is exactly 4 (one per regime sub-test). No new statistical tools beyond what is already in the kernel.
bias_self_audit.sunk_cost_note: Hard caps: max 2 holdout decryption events (currently 0 used) enforced by tools/holdout_access_ceremony.py:ceremony_should_refuse. Key loss policy in docs/operations/holdout_key_rotation.md = forced NO_GO with no rebuild. Q1 NO_GO rule fires regardless of effort sunk into the architecture.
bias_self_audit.recency_note: Bands and rules pre-committed BEFORE any P2 architecture-level evidence; cannot be rationalized post-hoc by recent in-development PF readings.
bias_self_audit.narrative_note: Verdict bands and Q1/I1 rules are mechanical and code-enforced. No narrative interpretation is required at verdict time; the DEC-ML-2.0-RE-GATE entry will state observed_lift, BCa CI, and machine-derived verdict.
bias_self_audit.survivorship_note: Primary endpoint cohort is a fixed survivor set; the secondary cohort retains all 11 candidates (including fragiles) as a survivorship-bias check. Q1 rule fires only when fragile cohort is aggregately negative, ensuring survivorship doesn't paper over architectural fragility.

rationale: Amendment A1 to the Phase 1.6b scope: SEQUENTIAL FEATURES (1D-CNN over rolling windows of price/return inputs) ARE EXPLICITLY EXCLUDED from the Phase 2.0 PRIMARY ENDPOINT cohort. The Phase 2.0 verdict will be determined exclusively by the locked XGB-on-tabular-features architecture (BASELINE_FEATURE_COLUMNS in src/agentic_forex/ml/baseline_runner.py). Sequential-feature variants (1D-CNN; src/agentic_forex/ml/sequential_features.py once implemented) MAY be evaluated in parallel as DESCRIPTIVE secondary endpoints but their results CANNOT influence the Phase 2 GO/CONDITIONAL/NO_GO verdict. RATIONALE FOR EXCLUSION: (1) The 1.6 baseline + EX-1 MDE derivation are calibrated to the tabular feature set; introducing a sequential variant at evaluation time would require a separate sigma_cross computation, separate MDE band, separate Bonferroni family, and a separate pre-registration entry. (2) torch is excluded from the without-torch CI lane (tests-without-torch); admitting sequential features into the primary endpoint would require switching the lane and revalidating EX-5 torch determinism end-to-end. (3) Excluding sequential features from the binding endpoint preserves the apples-to-apples comparison against the 1.6 baseline and prevents architecture-shopping at verdict time. ENFORCEMENT: tests-without-torch CI lane stays green; assert_no_torch_import() in scripts/run_ml_baseline_comparison.py and tools/derive_mde.py continues to fail loudly if torch is transitively imported. The ml-p2 optional extra in pyproject.toml exists for the EX-5 deterministic 1D-CNN test ONLY; it is not installed in the primary CI lane. POST-PHASE-2 PATH: After the Phase 2 verdict is recorded in DEC-ML-2.0-RE-GATE, a separate phase (ML-3.0 or later) MAY pre-register a sequential-feature variant under its own DEC-ML-3.0-CANDIDATES + DEC-ML-3.0-TARGET pair with its own sealed holdout (HOLDOUT-ML-P3-YYYYMMDD).
torch_optional_extra_purpose: EX-5 deterministic 1D-CNN test only; not installed in tests-without-torch lane

bias_self_audit.confirmation_bias_note: Excluding sequential features at this gate prevents architecture-shopping AT verdict time (selecting whichever variant happens to look best on the holdout). The exclusion is pre-committed BEFORE Phase 2 evaluation, so it cannot be relaxed post-hoc to swap in a better-performing variant.
bias_self_audit.cherry_picking_note: The decision binds the primary endpoint to ONE feature set. A sequential variant cannot be cherry-picked into the primary at evaluation time. Any sequential evaluation is descriptive-only and clearly labelled.
bias_self_audit.anchoring_note: Exclusion is based on calibration scope (EX-1 sigma_cross was computed on tabular features only) and CI-lane integrity (without-torch lane), not on any anticipated sequential-feature performance.
bias_self_audit.complexity_creep_note: This amendment is a FENCE against complexity creep: it explicitly forbids importing torch into the primary endpoint pipeline, keeping the binding architecture as simple as the 1.6 baseline.
bias_self_audit.sunk_cost_note: If sequential-feature work was performed in Phase 1.6b expecting to be primary, that effort is sunk; this amendment commits to descriptive-only inclusion regardless. Any later-phase repromotion requires a fresh sealed holdout.
bias_self_audit.recency_note: The exclusion is independent of any recent sequential-feature prototype results; it is a categorical scope-fence.
bias_self_audit.narrative_note: The amendment rationale is a calibration-and-CI argument, not a narrative preference for tabular over sequential features.
bias_self_audit.survivorship_note: By keeping the primary endpoint on the same feature set as 1.6, the survivor cohort selected from 1.6 gates remains a valid pre-commitment under the same comparison rules; switching feature sets would invalidate the 1.6-derived survivor set.

rationale: ML-P2.0 primary holdout evaluation PRED-ML-2.0-MIDPOINT-1 filed
